response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack/web engineer with a broad polyglot background, but the role demands principal-level expertise in SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, and hardware-software co-design. Apart from a stint writing low-latency DLang services and brief C/C++ IDE work at National Instruments, nothing in the resume touches SIMD/MIMD architectures, GPU or accelerator workloads, deep-learning performance optimization, or silicon design feedback. The core required skills are essentially absent, so the fit for this specific role is very poor despite solid general engineering credentials.
strengths: Real low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching in DLang at millions of requests/sec)
Broad, long-tenured engineering career with strong testing and software craftsmanship practices
Some C/C++ and hardware-adjacent exposure (LabWindows/CVI tooling, robotic arms, audio wave-field synthesis thesis)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, system architecture design, or hardware-software co-design experience
No AI/deep-learning workload, GPU architecture, or accelerator performance-optimization background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack product engineering, not performance architecture at principal level

key_factors.factor: Missing SoC/IP modeling and hardware architecture experience
No AI accelerator or deep-learning workload expertise
Low-latency systems programming and performance tuning background
